question_paper_html:
Annual Exam - 2025

Code: 56/5/3 (set-3)

  1. Please check that this question paper contains 23 printed pages.
  2. Please check that this question paper contains 33 questions.
  3. Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  4. Please write down the serial number of the question in the answer-book at the given place before attempting it.
  5. 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the candidates will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

CHEMISTRY (Theory)

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:70

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions carefully and follow them:

  1. This question paper contains 33 questions. All questions are compulsory.
  2. This question paper is divided into five sections - Section A, B, C, D and E.
  3. Section A - questions number 1 to 16 are multiple choice type questions. Each question carries 1 mark.
  4. Section B - questions number 17 to 21 are very short answer type questions. Each question carries 2 marks.
  5. Section C - questions number 22 to 28 are short answer type questions. Each question carries 3 marks.
  6. Section D - questions number 29 and 30 are case-based questions. Each question carries 4 marks.
  7. Section E - questions number 31 to 33 are long answer type questions. Each question carries 5 marks.
  8. There is no overall choice given in the question paper. However, an internal choice has been provided in few questions in all the sections except Section -A.
  9. Kindly note that there is a separate question paper for Visually Impaired candidates.
  10. Use of calculator is NOT allowed.
    You may use the following values of physical constants wherever necessary:

SECTION A

Questions no. 1 to 16 are Multiple Choice type Questions, carrying 1 mark each. (16{tex}\times{/tex}1=16)
1.

{tex}{CH}_{3} {CH}_{2} {CHO}{/tex} and {tex}{CH}_{3} {CH}_{2} {COOH}{/tex} can be distinguished by:

a. Sodium bicarbonate test
b. Hinsberg test
c. Iodoform test
d. Lucas test
1
2.

While doing qualitative analysis in chemistry lab, Abhishek added yellow coloured potassium chromate solution into a test tube. He was surprised to see the colour of the solution changing immediately to orange. He realised that the test tube was not clean and contained a few drops of some liquid. Which of the following substances will be the most likely liquid to be present in the test tube before adding potassium chromate solution?

a. Sodium hydrogen carbonate solution
b. Methyl orange solution
c. Sodium hydroxide solution
d. {tex}{HCl}{/tex} solution
1
3.

The role of a catalyst is to change:

a. Gibbs energy of reaction
b. enthalpy of reaction
c. activation energy of reaction
d. quilibrium constant
1
4.

Which of the following molecules is chiral in nature?

a. 1-chloropropane
b. 2-chloropropane
c. 1-chlorobutane
d. 2-chlorobutane
1
5.

{tex}{CH}_{3} {CH}_{2} {OH}{/tex} can be converted to {tex}{CH}_{3} {CHO}{/tex} by:

a. catalytic hydrogenation
b. treatment with {tex}{LiAlH}_{4}{/tex}
c. treatment with PCC
d. treatment with {tex}{KMnO}_{4}{/tex}
1
6.

The IUPAC name for {tex}{CH}_{3}-{CH}_{2}-{N}\left({CH}_{3}\right)-{CH}_{2}-{CH}_{2}-{CH}_{3}{/tex} is:

a. N-methylpentan-2-amine
b. N-ethyl-N-methylpropan-1-amine
c. N,N-diethylpropan-1-amine
d. N,N-dimethylpropan-1-amine
1
7.

A plot between concentration of reactant [R] and time '{tex}t{/tex}' is shown below. Which of the given order of reaction is indicated by the graph?

a. Third order
b. Second order
c. First order
d. Zero order
1
8.

The treatment of ethyl bromide with alcoholic silver nitrite gives:

a. ethyl nitrite
b. nitroethane
c. nitromethane
d. ethene
1
9.

Which of the following aqueous solutions will have the highest freezing point?

a. 1.0 M KCl
b. {tex}1.0 \ {M} \ {Na}_{2} {SO}_{4}{/tex}
c. 1.0 M Glucose
d. {tex}1.0 \ {M} \ {AlCl}_{3}{/tex}
1
10.

Which of the following aldehydes will undergo Cannizzaro reaction?

a.
b. {tex}\left({CH}_{3}\right)_{3} {C} {CHO}{/tex}
c. {tex}{CH}_{3}-{CH}_{2}-{CHO}{/tex}
d.
1
11.

In which of the following groups are both ions coloured in aqueous solution?

  1. {tex}{Cu}^{+}{/tex}
  2. {tex}{Ti}^{4+}{/tex}
  3. {tex}{Co}^{2+}{/tex}
  4. {tex}{Fe}^{2+}{/tex}

[Atomic number{tex}: {Cu}=29, {Ti}=22, {Co}=27, {Fe}=26{/tex}]

a. I and II
b. II and III
c. III and IV
d. I and IV
1
12.

Match the type of cell given in Column I with their use given in Column II.

Column I Column II
i. Lead storage cell a. Wall clock
ii. Mercury cell b. Apollo Space Programme
iii. Dry cell c. Wrist watch
iv. Fuel cell d. Inverter
a. (i) - (a), (ii) - (b), (iii) - (c), (iv) - (d)
b. (i) - (d), (ii) - (c), (iii) - (a), (iv) - (b)
c. (i) - (c), (ii) - (d), (iii) - (b), (iv) - (a)
d. (i) - (b), (ii) - (a), (iii) - (d), (iv) - (c)
1

For Questions number 13 to 16, two statements are given - one labelled as Assertion (A) and the other labelled as Reason (R). Select the correct answer to these questions from the codes (A), (B), (C) and (D) as given below.

  1. Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason (R) is the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
  2. Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, but Reason (R) is not the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
  3. Assertion (A) is true, but Reason (R) is false.
  4. Assertion (A) is false, but Reason (R) is true.
13.

Assertion (A): The solubility of aldehydes and ketones in water decreases with increase in size of the alkyl group.
Reason (R): Aldehydes and ketones have dipole-dipole interaction.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1
14.

Assertion (A): The boiling points of alkyl halides decrease in the order {tex}{RI} \gt {RBr} \gt {RCl} \gt {RF}{/tex}.
Reason (R): The van der Waals forces of attraction decrease in the order {tex}{RI} \gt {RBr} \gt {RCl} \gt {RF}{/tex}.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1
15.

Assertion (A): For measuring the resistance of an ionic solution an AC source is used.
Reason (R): Concentration of ionic solution will change if the DC source is used.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1
16.

Assertion (A): Henry's law constant ({tex}{K}_{{H}}{/tex}) decreases with increase in temperature.
Reason (R): As the temperature increases, solubility of gases in liquids decreases.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1

SECTION B
17.(i)

Calculate the elevation of boiling point of a solution when 3 g of {tex}{CaCl}_{2}{/tex} (Molar mass {tex}=111 {~g} {~mol}^{-1}{/tex}) was dissolved in 260 g of water, assuming that {tex}{CaCl}_{2}{/tex} undergoes complete dissociation. ({tex}{K}_{{b}}{/tex} for water {tex}=0.52 {~K} {~kg} {~mol}^{-1}{/tex})

 2

OR
(ii)

Liquids 'X' and 'Y' form an ideal solution. The vapour pressure of pure 'X' and pure 'Y' are 120 mm Hg and 160 mm Hg respectively. Calculate the vapour pressure of the solution containing equal moles of 'X' and 'Y'.

 2
18.
Concentration of KCl solution in {tex}{mol} / {L}{/tex} Conductivity at 298.15 K in {tex}{S}\ {cm}^{-1}{/tex} Molar Conductivity at {tex}298.15 {~K} \ {in} \ {S} \ {cm}^2 {~mol}^{-1}{/tex}
1.000 0.1113 111.3
0.100 0.0129 129.0
0.010 0.00141 141.0

Based on the data given above, give plausible reason for the variation of conductivity and molar conductivity with concentration.

 2
19.
  1. Give any two differences between order and molecularity of a reaction.
  2. For a reaction {tex}{X}+{Y} \rightarrow {Z}{/tex}, in which both X and Y follow first order kinetics; if the concentration of {tex}X{/tex} is increased 2 times and concentration of Y is increased 3 times, how does it affect the rate of reaction?
 2
20.

Write the mechanism of dehydration of ethyl alcohol with conc. {tex}{H}_{2} {SO}_{4}{/tex} at 443 K.

 2
21.
  1. Carboxylic acid is more acidic than phenol. Give reason.
  2. Give a chemical test to distinguish between benzaldehyde and acetophenone.
 2

SECTION C
22.(i)

Shweta mixed two liquids {tex}A{/tex} and {tex}B{/tex} of 10 mL each. After mixing, the volume of the solution was found to be {tex}20 \cdot 2 {~mL}{/tex}.

  1. Why was there a volume change after mixing the liquids?
  2. Will there be an increase or decrease of temperature after mixing?
  3. Give one example for this type of solution.
 3

OR
(ii)
  1. How does sprinkling of salt help in clearing the snow covered roads in hilly areas?
  2. What happens when red blood cells are kept in {tex}0 \cdot 5 \%{/tex} (mass/vol) NaCl solution? Justify your answer.
  3. Write an application of reverse osmosis.
 3
23.

For the reaction {tex}{A}+{B} \rightarrow{/tex} Products, the following initial rates were obtained at various initial concentrations of reactants:

Sl. No. {tex}[A] / {mol} {L}^{-1}{/tex} {tex}[B] / {mol} {L}^{-1}{/tex} Initial rate {tex}/ {mol} {L}^{-1} {~s}^{-1}{/tex}
1 0.1 0.1 0.05
2 0.2 0.1 0.10
3 0.1 0.2 0.05

Determine the order of the reaction with respect to A and B and overall order of the reaction.

 3
24.
  1. Write the name and structure of a hexadentate ligand.
  2. Why is {tex}\left[{Ni}({CN})_{4}\right]^{2-}{/tex} square planar while {tex}\left[{Ni}({CO})_{4}\right]{/tex} is tetrahedral?
    [Atomic number : {tex}{Ni}=28{/tex}]
 3
25.

Account for the following:

  1. Haloarenes are less reactive towards nucleophilic substitution reactions.
  2. {tex}p{/tex}-dichlorobenzene has higher melting point than ortho and meta isomers.
  3. Tertiary alkyl halides are least reactive towards {tex}{S}_{{N}} 2{/tex} reaction.
 3
26.
  1. Write the chemical equation for the following:
    1. Preparation of phenol from cumene
    2. Nitration of anisole
  2. Complete the following:
    {tex}\left({CH}_{3}\right)_{3} {C}-{O}-{CH}_{3}+{HI} \rightarrow{/tex}
 3
27.

Write the products formed in the following reactions:

  1. {tex}{CH}_{3} {CHO}+{NH}_{2} {CONHNH}_{2} \rightarrow{/tex}
  2. {tex}{CH}_{3} {CHO} \xrightarrow{\text { dil. } {NaOH}}{/tex}
  3.  {tex}{CH}_{3} {COOH} \xrightarrow[{H}_{2} {O}]{{Cl}_{2} / \text { redP }}{/tex}
 3
28.
  1. Name the vitamin which is responsible for coagulation of blood.
  2. What is meant by denaturation of protein? Give an example.
 3

SECTION D

The following questions are case-based questions. Read the case carefully and answer the questions that follow.
29.

Carbohydrates are polyhydroxy aldehydes or ketones that represent enormous structural diversity in terms of the arrangement of atoms in space, resulting in hundreds of stereoisomers. Although the chemical properties of most stereoisomers may not be very different, their metabolic rate and utilization in biological systems is significantly different and known to influence the overall carbohydrate metabolism. Structural variants, which arise due to a different arrangement of atoms in three-dimensional space are known as stereoisomers. The number of stereoisomers can be theoretically estimated by using the formula 2 n, where '{tex}n{/tex}' is the number of stereocenters or asymmetric (chiral) carbon atoms in a molecule. Out of these stereoisomers, there are some structures, which are mirror images of each other, and they are referred to as enantiomers.
Answer the following questions:

  1. Give chemical reactions to show the presence of an aldehydic group and straight chain in glucose. (2)
    1. Define anomers. (1)

      OR

    2. Draw the structure of {tex}\beta{/tex}-D-Glucopyranose. (1)
  3. Sucrose is known as invert sugar. Explain. (1)
 4
30.

Werner's coordination theory in 1893 was the first attempt to explain the bonding in coordination complexes. It must be remembered that this theory was put forward before the electron had been discovered by J.J. Thomson in 1897, and before the electronic theory of valency. Werner did not have any of the modern instrumental techniques and all his studies were made using simple experimental techniques. Werner was able to explain the nature of bonding in complexes and he concluded that in complexes, the metal shows two different sorts of valency: primary and secondary. Primary valences are normally ionisable whereas secondary valences are non ionisable.
Answer the following questions:

  1. One mole of {tex}{CrCl}_{3} \cdot 4 {H}_{2} {O}{/tex} precipitates one mole of AgCl when treated with excess of {tex}{AgNO}_{3}{/tex} solution. Write (i) the structural formula of the complex, and (ii) the secondary valency of Cr. (2)
  2. What is the difference between a complex and a double salt? (1)
    1. Arrange the following complexes in the increasing order of conductivity of their solution: (1)
      {tex}\left[{Cr}\left({NH}_{3}\right)_{3} {Cl}_{3}\right], \left[{Cr}\left({NH}_{3}\right)_{6}\right] {Cl}_{3},{/tex} {tex} \left[{Cr}\left({NH}_{3}\right)_{5} {Cl}_{1}\right] {Cl}_{2}{/tex}

      OR

    2. Write two differences between primary and secondary valences in coordination compounds. (1)
 4

SECTION E
31.(i)
  1. When pyrolusite ore is fused with KOH, in presence of air, a dark green coloured product 'A' is obtained which changes to purple coloured compound 'B' in acidic medium.
    1. Write the formulae of 'A' and 'B'.
    2. Write the ionic equation for the reaction when compound 'B' reacts with {tex}{Fe}^{2+}{/tex} in acidic medium.
  2. Give reasons:
    1. {tex}{Ce}^{4+}{/tex} in aqueous solution is a good oxidising agent.
    2. The actinoid contraction is greater from element to element than lanthanoid contraction.
    3. {tex}{E}_{{Zn}^{2+} / {Zn}}^{\circ}{/tex} value is more negative than expected, whereas {tex}{E}_{{Cu}^{2+} / {Cu}}^{\circ}{/tex} is positive.
 5

OR
(ii)
  1. While studying the periodic properties, Arti came across an abnormal behaviour in the atomic size of Hf. She found that, even though Hf is placed below Zr in the same group, both have almost similar atomic sizes.
    1. Which phenomenon is responsible for the above behaviour? Define it.
    2. Mention any other consequence of the above phenomenon.
  2. Give reasons for the following:
    1. Transition metals exhibit catalytic properties.
    2. Transition metals have high enthalpy of atomisation.
    3. Sc is a transition element, while Zn is not.
 5
32.(i)
  1. For a galvanic cell, the following half reactions are given. Decide, which will remain as reduction reaction and which will be reversed to become an oxidation reaction. Give reason for your answer.
    1. {tex}{Cr}^{3+}+3 {e}^{-} \rightarrow \operatorname{Cr}({s}) ; {E}^{\circ}=-0 \cdot 74 {~V}{/tex}
    2. {tex}{Fe}^{2+}+2 {e}^{-} \rightarrow {Fe}({s}) ; {E}^{\circ}=-0 \cdot 44 {~V}{/tex}
  2. Represent the cell in which the following reaction takes place:
    {tex}{Mg}({s})+2 {Ag}^{+}(0 \cdot 001 {M}){/tex}{tex} \rightarrow {Mg}^{2+}(0 \cdot 100 {M})+2 {Ag}({s}){/tex}
    Calculate {tex}{E}_{\text {cell }}{/tex} if {tex}{E}_{\text {cell }}^{\circ}=3 \cdot 17 {~V} .(\log 10=1){/tex}
 5

OR
(ii)
  1. State Kohlrausch's law. Give any two applications of it.
  2. {tex}\wedge_{{m}}^{\circ} {NH}_{4} {Cl}, \wedge_{{m}}^{\circ} {NaOH}{/tex} and {tex}\wedge_{{m}}^{\circ} {NaCl}{/tex} are 129.8, 217.4, and {tex}108.9 {~S} {~cm}^{2} {~mol}^{-1}{/tex} respectively. Molar conductivity of {tex}1 \times 10^{-2} {M}{/tex} solution of {tex}{NH}_{4} {OH}{/tex} is {tex}9 \cdot 33 {~S} {~cm}^{2} {~mol}^{-1}{/tex}. Calculate the degree of dissociation ({tex}\alpha{/tex}) of {tex}{NH}_{4} {OH}{/tex} solution at this concentration.
 5
33.(i)
  1. In a chemistry practical class, the teacher gave his students an amine 'X' having molecular formula {tex}{C}_{2} {H}_{7} {~N}{/tex}, and asked the students to identify the type of amine. One of the students, Neeta, observed that it reacts with {tex}{C}_{6} {H}_{5} {SO}_{2} {Cl}{/tex}, to give a compound which dissolves in NaOH solution. Can you help Neeta to identify the compound 'X'?
  2. Arrange the following in the increasing order of their {tex}{pK}_{{b}}{/tex} value in aqueous phase:
    {tex}{C}_{6} {H}_{5} {NH}_{2},\left({CH}_{3}\right)_{2} {NH}, {/tex}{tex}{NH}_{3}, {CH}_{3} {NH}_{2},\left({CH}_{3}\right)_{3} {~N}{/tex}
  3. Aniline on nitration gives considerable amount of meta product along with ortho and para products. Why?
  4. Convert aniline to:
    1. p-bromoaniline
    2. phenol
 5

OR
(ii)
  1. Arun heated a mixture of ethylamine and {tex}{CHCl}_{3}{/tex} with ethanolic KOH, which forms a foul smelling gas. Write the chemical equation involved.
  2. Identify A and B in the following reactions :
  3. Convert aniline to:
    1. benzene
    2. sulphanilic acid
 5

For the reaction {tex}{A}+{B} \rightarrow{/tex} Products, the following initial rates were obtained at various initial concentrations of reactants:

Sl. No. {tex}[A] / {mol} {L}^{-1}{/tex} {tex}[B] / {mol} {L}^{-1}{/tex} Initial rate {tex}/ {mol} {L}^{-1} {~s}^{-1}{/tex}
1 0.1 0.1 0.05
2 0.2 0.1 0.10
3 0.1 0.2 0.05

Determine the order of the reaction with respect to A and B and overall order of the reaction.

Solution:

{tex} \text { Rate }={k}[{~A}]^{{x}}[{~B}]^{{y}} {/tex}
{tex} \text { Eq. } 1 \text { Rate }_1={k}(0.1)^{{x}}(0.1)^{{y}}=5.0 \times 10^{-2} {/tex}
{tex} \text { Eq. } 2 \text { Rate }_2={k}(0.2)^{{x}}(0.1)^{{y}}=1.0 \times 10^{-1} {/tex}
{tex} \text { Eq. } 3 \text { Rate }_3={k}(0.1)^{{x}}(0.2)^{{y}}=5.0 \times 10^{-2} {/tex}
{tex} \frac{0.1}{0.5}=\frac{k \times 0.2^x \times 0.1^y}{k \times 0.1^x \times 0.1^y} {/tex}
{tex} \text { Hence } {x}=1 {/tex}
{tex} \frac{0.05}{0.05}=\frac{k \times 0.1^x \times 0.2^y}{k \times 0.1^x \times 0.1^y} {/tex}
{tex} \text { Hence } {y}=0 {/tex}
{tex} \text { Rate }={k}[{~A}]^1[{~B}]^0 {/tex}
{tex} \text { Overall order }=1 {/tex}
